collector.issue.permission.description: Auditex could not read the requested Microsoft 365 surface with the supplied identity.
collector.issue.permission.impact: The report has a confirmed evidence gap for this area, so the related control cannot be asserted from this run.
collector.issue.permission.remediation: Rerun with the minimum read permission required for the blocked surface, or exclude that surface from the agreed scope.
collector.issue.permission.expected_value: The collector can read the requested surface.

collector.issue.service.description: The collector reached a tenant surface that is absent, disabled, not provisioned, or temporarily unavailable.
collector.issue.service.impact: Evidence for the affected service remains partial until the tenant state or service availability changes.
collector.issue.service.remediation: Confirm licensing and service availability, then rerun only the affected collector where appropriate.
collector.issue.service.expected_value: The target service surface responds successfully.

collector.issue.collector.description: A collector finished with an operational error unrelated to normal permission or service-state handling.
collector.issue.collector.impact: The affected evidence set may be missing, incomplete, or unsuitable for comparison.
collector.issue.collector.remediation: Inspect the collector diagnostic row, fix the runtime or input condition, and rerun the collector.
collector.issue.collector.expected_value: The collector completes without runtime errors.

sharepoint.broad_link.description: SharePoint or OneDrive evidence indicates a sharing link with broad audience reach.
sharepoint.broad_link.impact: Content may be reachable by users outside the intended collaboration boundary.
sharepoint.broad_link.remediation: Reduce the sharing scope, remove stale broad links, and retest affected sites.
sharepoint.broad_link.expected_value: External and organization-wide links are absent or explicitly approved.

app_consent.high_privilege.description: Application consent evidence shows sensitive scopes or weak ownership governance.
app_consent.high_privilege.impact: Over-privileged or unowned application consent can become a durable path to tenant data exposure.
app_consent.high_privilege.remediation: Review the consent grant, remove unused scopes, and assign accountable owners before accepting the application.
app_consent.high_privilege.expected_value: Sensitive app grants are approved, current, and owned.

app_credentials.secret_expired.description: An application credential (secret or certificate) has already expired.
app_credentials.secret_expired.impact: An expired credential indicates either an unmaintained app or a credential that should be rotated and removed; leaving expired material attached invites stale state and reuse risk.
app_credentials.secret_expired.remediation: Remove the expired credential, rotate to a fresh secret or certificate, and confirm the application is still required.
references: Microsoft Entra application credential rotation guidance
app_credentials.secret_expired.expected_value: All attached credentials are within their validity window.

app_credentials.secret_expiring.description: An application credential will expire within 30 days.
app_credentials.secret_expiring.impact: Imminent credential expiry can break automation if not rotated proactively.
app_credentials.secret_expiring.remediation: Rotate the secret or certificate, deploy the new value, and remove the soon-to-expire credential.
references: Microsoft Entra application credential rotation guidance
app_credentials.secret_expiring.expected_value: Credentials are rotated before they expire.

app_credentials.redirect_insecure.description: Application has an HTTP redirect URI pointing at a non-localhost host.
app_credentials.redirect_insecure.impact: HTTP redirect URIs can leak authorization codes and tokens to the network and bypass replay protection.
app_credentials.redirect_insecure.remediation: Replace HTTP redirect URIs with HTTPS equivalents and remove unused redirects.
app_credentials.redirect_insecure.expected_value: All non-localhost redirect URIs use HTTPS.

app_credentials.no_owner.description: Application registration has no listed owners.
app_credentials.no_owner.impact: Apps without owners drift out of governance and become hard to retire or rotate.
app_credentials.no_owner.remediation: Assign at least one accountable owner; remove the app if it is no longer required.
app_credentials.no_owner.expected_value: Each tenant-owned application has a documented owner.

app_credentials.multi_tenant_audience.description: Application sign-in audience permits multi-tenant or personal Microsoft accounts.
app_credentials.multi_tenant_audience.impact: Multi-tenant or personal-account audiences broaden the population that can interact with the app and can be misused if redirect URIs or scopes are weak.
app_credentials.multi_tenant_audience.remediation: Restrict the audience to single-tenant unless multi-tenant is explicitly required and reviewed.
app_credentials.multi_tenant_audience.expected_value: Sign-in audience is 'AzureADMyOrg' for tenant-owned apps unless multi-tenant is justified.

app_credentials.certificate_expired.description: An application certificate (keyCredential) has already expired.
app_credentials.certificate_expired.impact: Once a cert expires, downstream Microsoft and partner authentication fails; lingering expired certs also indicate weak rotation hygiene and unmaintained apps.
app_credentials.certificate_expired.remediation: Remove the expired certificate, issue a fresh one with a documented validity window, and confirm the application is still required.
references: Microsoft Entra application credential rotation guidance
app_credentials.certificate_expired.expected_value: All keyCredentials are within their validity window.

app_credentials.certificate_expiring.description: An application certificate (keyCredential) will expire within 30 days.
app_credentials.certificate_expiring.impact: Certificate rotation has more lead time than secret rotation; flagging at 30 days lets the operator schedule reissuance before authentication breaks.
app_credentials.certificate_expiring.remediation: Reissue the certificate before expiry, deploy the new value, and remove the soon-to-expire entry.
references: Microsoft Entra application credential rotation guidance
app_credentials.certificate_expiring.expected_value: keyCredentials are rotated at least 30 days before they expire.

app_credentials.secret_long_validity.description: An application secret either has no end_date_time or a validity window that exceeds two years.
app_credentials.secret_long_validity.impact: Long-lived or open-ended secrets violate cryptographic hygiene, magnify blast radius if leaked, and bypass Entra's default 24-month cap. They are a recurring root cause of supply-chain compromise.
app_credentials.secret_long_validity.remediation: Replace with a secret bounded to 6-12 months (or move to certificate auth) and rotate on a schedule.

app_credentials.credential_dormant.description: Application has not signed in for more than 365 days (signInActivity observed and null).
app_credentials.credential_dormant.impact: Stale applications expand the attack surface — credentials persist after the workload is gone, owners forget the app exists, and consent grants outlive their purpose.
app_credentials.credential_dormant.remediation: Confirm the application is still required; if not, retire it (delete, or disable and document) and revoke consent grants.
app_credentials.credential_dormant.expected_value: Application has signed in within the last year, or has been retired.

cross_tenant_access.default_b2b_collabor.description: Default cross-tenant access policy allows inbound B2B Collaboration from any external tenant.
cross_tenant_access.default_b2b_collabor.impact: Open inbound B2B Collaboration lets users from any external tenant be invited to resources in your tenant without per-partner scoping or trust requirements; common phishing-driven account-takeover precursor.
cross_tenant_access.default_b2b_collabor.remediation: Set the default B2B Collaboration inbound access to 'blocked' and enable per-partner allow-lists with inbound trust requirements (MFA, compliant device).
references: Microsoft Entra cross-tenant access default policy guidance
cross_tenant_access.default_b2b_collabor.expected_value: Default inbound B2B Collaboration is 'blocked' or scoped to specific partners.

cross_tenant_access.default_b2b_direct_c.description: Default cross-tenant access policy allows outbound B2B Direct Connect to any external tenant.
cross_tenant_access.default_b2b_direct_c.impact: Open outbound Direct Connect lets your users participate in shared channels in any external tenant; sensitive content can flow out without partner-specific governance.
cross_tenant_access.default_b2b_direct_c.remediation: Set default outbound Direct Connect to 'blocked' and enable per-partner where shared-channel collaboration is required.
references: Microsoft Entra cross-tenant access default policy guidance
cross_tenant_access.default_b2b_direct_c.expected_value: Default outbound B2B Direct Connect is 'blocked' or scoped per partner.

cross_tenant_access.auto_user_consent_in.description: Cross-tenant access policy enables automatic user consent for inbound external users.
cross_tenant_access.auto_user_consent_in.impact: Automatic user consent skips the per-app consent prompt for external users, removing a key data-sharing checkpoint and enabling silent over-permissioned flows.
cross_tenant_access.auto_user_consent_in.remediation: Disable automatic user consent inbound and require explicit user or admin consent for external app access.
references: Microsoft Entra automatic user consent settings

cross_tenant_access.auto_user_consent_ou.description: Cross-tenant access policy enables automatic user consent for outbound interactions with external apps.
cross_tenant_access.auto_user_consent_ou.impact: Outbound auto-consent lets your users grant access to data hosted in external tenants without an explicit consent prompt; weakens data-egress visibility.
cross_tenant_access.auto_user_consent_ou.remediation: Disable automatic user consent outbound; require an explicit consent step or admin approval for external app access.
references: Microsoft Entra automatic user consent settings

cross_tenant_access.default_b2b_direct_c.description: Default cross-tenant access policy allows inbound B2B Direct Connect from any tenant.
cross_tenant_access.default_b2b_direct_c.impact: Inbound B2B Direct Connect grants external identities elevated collaboration capabilities (shared channels, native Teams identity). Enabling it tenant-wide bypasses partner-specific scoping.
cross_tenant_access.default_b2b_direct_c.remediation: Set the default B2B Direct Connect inbound access to 'blocked' and enable it explicitly per partner with inbound trust requirements (MFA, compliant device).
cross_tenant_access.default_b2b_direct_c.expected_value: Default inbound B2B Direct Connect is blocked; partner-specific allow-lists are used.

cross_tenant_access.default_b2b_collabor.description: Default cross-tenant access policy permits outbound B2B Collaboration to any tenant.
cross_tenant_access.default_b2b_collabor.impact: Tenant users can be invited into any external tenant by default, increasing the surface for guest-account misuse and conditional access bypass.
cross_tenant_access.default_b2b_collabor.remediation: Restrict default outbound B2B Collaboration and approve outbound trust per partner.
cross_tenant_access.default_b2b_collabor.expected_value: Default outbound B2B Collaboration is blocked except for approved partners.

cross_tenant_access.partner_inbound_no_m.description: Partner-specific inbound B2B Direct Connect is allowed but inbound MFA trust is not accepted.
cross_tenant_access.partner_inbound_no_m.impact: Without inbound MFA trust, partner identities can authenticate with weaker controls than tenant users, undermining MFA enforcement on shared resources.
cross_tenant_access.partner_inbound_no_m.remediation: Enable inbound MFA trust acceptance for partners that allow B2B Direct Connect, or block direct connect for partners that cannot honour MFA trust.
cross_tenant_access.partner_inbound_no_m.expected_value: Partners with inbound B2B Direct Connect honour inbound MFA trust.

mailbox_forwarding.external_inbox_rule.description: An inbox rule forwards or redirects mail to a recipient outside the tenant's accepted domains.
mailbox_forwarding.external_inbox_rule.impact: External-forwarding inbox rules are the leading indicator of business email compromise (BEC) and active mailbox takeover.
mailbox_forwarding.external_inbox_rule.remediation: Disable the rule, investigate the affected mailbox for compromise, force a password reset, revoke sessions, and review sign-in logs for anomalies.
mailbox_forwarding.external_inbox_rule.expected_value: No inbox rule forwards mail to external recipients.

mailbox_forwarding.hide_from_user.description: An inbox rule moves or deletes mail in a way that hides it from the user (RSS Feeds, Junk, Deleted Items, or delete action).
mailbox_forwarding.hide_from_user.impact: Hide-from-user inbox rules are a common BEC technique to suppress visibility of fraud, replies, or forensic alerts.
mailbox_forwarding.hide_from_user.remediation: Disable the rule, audit the mailbox for compromise, and review recent inbox-rule mutations in the unified audit log.
mailbox_forwarding.hide_from_user.expected_value: Inbox rules do not silently hide messages from the user.

dns_posture.spf_missing.description: No SPF (Sender Policy Framework) TXT record was discovered for a verified tenant domain.
dns_posture.spf_missing.impact: Without SPF, receivers cannot validate authorized senders for the domain, which raises the risk of spoofing and phishing of the tenant's brand.
dns_posture.spf_missing.remediation: Publish an SPF record at the apex (for example, 'v=spf1 include:spf.protection.outlook.com -all') and verify external receivers honour it.
dns_posture.spf_missing.expected_value: SPF record present with a hard-fail (-all) qualifier.

dns_posture.spf_passthrough.description: SPF record terminates with a permissive '+all' or '?all' qualifier.
dns_posture.spf_passthrough.impact: Pass-through SPF accepts mail from any sender, defeating the purpose of SPF and enabling spoofing.
dns_posture.spf_passthrough.remediation: Replace the trailing qualifier with '-all' (hard fail) or '~all' (soft fail) and revalidate.

dns_posture.dmarc_missing.description: No DMARC record was discovered at the _dmarc subdomain of a verified tenant domain.
dns_posture.dmarc_missing.impact: Without DMARC, receivers cannot enforce alignment between SPF/DKIM and the From header, leaving the domain spoofable.
dns_posture.dmarc_missing.remediation: Publish a DMARC record (start with 'v=DMARC1; p=none; rua=mailto:dmarc@<domain>'), monitor reports, and progress to 'p=quarantine' or 'p=reject'.
dns_posture.dmarc_missing.expected_value: DMARC record present with an enforced policy.

dns_posture.dmarc_monitor_only.description: DMARC policy is set to 'p=none' (monitor-only) and is not enforcing alignment.
dns_posture.dmarc_monitor_only.impact: Monitor-only DMARC reports failures but does not prevent spoofed mail from being delivered.
dns_posture.dmarc_monitor_only.remediation: After validating SPF and DKIM via DMARC reports, raise the policy to 'p=quarantine' and then 'p=reject'.

dns_posture.dkim_missing.description: No DKIM selectors were discovered for a verified tenant domain.
dns_posture.dkim_missing.impact: Without DKIM, mail receivers cannot cryptographically authenticate messages, weakening DMARC alignment.
dns_posture.dkim_missing.remediation: Enable DKIM signing for the domain in Microsoft 365 (selector1/selector2) and publish the corresponding CNAME records.
dns_posture.dkim_missing.expected_value: At least one DKIM selector resolves with a valid public key.

dns_posture.spf_multiple_records.description: More than one SPF (v=spf1) TXT record is published at the domain apex.
dns_posture.spf_multiple_records.impact: RFC 7208 §4.5 mandates exactly one SPF record; multiple v=spf1 records cause receivers to return PermError, leaving the domain effectively unauthenticated.
dns_posture.spf_multiple_records.remediation: Merge SPF policy into a single record using nested 'include:' mechanisms and remove the redundant TXT entries.
dns_posture.spf_multiple_records.expected_value: Exactly one v=spf1 record at the domain apex.

dns_posture.dmarc_pct_partial.description: DMARC policy is enforcing (p=quarantine or p=reject) but only on a fraction of mail (pct<100).
dns_posture.dmarc_pct_partial.impact: Partial enforcement leaves a documented bypass: receivers apply the enforcing policy probabilistically, so spoofed mail can still be delivered to users.
dns_posture.dmarc_pct_partial.remediation: Once SPF/DKIM coverage is healthy, raise pct to 100 (or omit pct, which defaults to 100) so the policy applies to every message.

dns_posture.dmarc_rua_invalid.description: DMARC rua= aggregate-report URI list contains entries that are neither well-formed mailto: addresses nor https:// URLs.
dns_posture.dmarc_rua_invalid.impact: Invalid URIs prevent DMARC report delivery; the operator loses visibility into spoofing attempts and SPF/DKIM alignment failures.
dns_posture.dmarc_rua_invalid.remediation: Replace malformed entries with mailto:<address>@<domain> or https://<endpoint>; remove bare addresses missing the mailto: scheme and HTTP (clear-text) endpoints.
dns_posture.dmarc_rua_invalid.expected_value: All rua= URIs use mailto: with a valid address or https://.

dns_posture.bimi_logo_insecure.description: BIMI l= logo URL is not served over HTTPS.
dns_posture.bimi_logo_insecure.impact: BIMI consumers (e.g., Gmail, Yahoo) require HTTPS-served SVG logos; HTTP delivery prevents brand-indicator rendering and exposes logo fetches to tampering.
dns_posture.bimi_logo_insecure.remediation: Host the BIMI SVG over HTTPS and update the l= tag to the https:// URL.
references: BIMI draft (Brand Indicators for Message Identification)
dns_posture.bimi_logo_insecure.expected_value: BIMI l= URL uses the https:// scheme.

service_health.active_service_issue.description: An active Microsoft 365 service issue is currently affecting the tenant or one of its services.
service_health.active_service_issue.impact: Active service degradations or interruptions may invalidate evidence collected during the audit window and can mask other anomalies; operators should correlate the audit findings with the timeline of the service issue.
service_health.active_service_issue.remediation: Track the issue in the Microsoft 365 admin center service-health board, document any audit-evidence gaps caused by the outage, and re-run affected collectors once the service is restored.
service_health.active_service_issue.expected_value: No active service issues during the audit window.

external_identity.broad_guest_invite_pol.description: Authorization policy allows guest invitations from anyone (including unauthenticated members).
external_identity.broad_guest_invite_pol.impact: Tenant-wide ``allowInvitesFrom=everyone`` lets any member invite arbitrary external identities into the tenant, expanding the attack surface and bypassing partner-specific governance.
external_identity.broad_guest_invite_pol.remediation: Restrict ``allowInvitesFrom`` to ``adminsAndGuestInviters`` (CIS-recommended) or ``adminsGuestInvitersAndAllMembers`` with reviewer rotation; document who can invite externals.
external_identity.broad_guest_invite_pol.expected_value: allowInvitesFrom restricted to adminsAndGuestInviters or stricter.

consent_policy.admin_consent_workflow_di.description: Admin consent request workflow is disabled for users requiring app permissions beyond the user-consent allowlist.
consent_policy.admin_consent_workflow_di.impact: Without the admin-consent workflow, end-users either consent unilaterally to risky scopes or are blocked entirely with no escalation path — both outcomes erode tenant governance over OAuth grants.
consent_policy.admin_consent_workflow_di.remediation: Enable the admin consent request workflow (Microsoft Entra → Enterprise applications → Consent and permissions → Admin consent settings) and assign reviewers with a documented SLA.
consent_policy.admin_consent_workflow_di.expected_value: Admin consent request workflow enabled with named reviewers.
